name: Do I really need permits for a kitchen remodel in Pacifica or Daly City?
headline: Do I really need permits for a kitchen remodel in Pacifica or Daly City?

mainEntity.name: Do I really need permits for a kitchen remodel in Pacifica or Daly City?
mainEntity.text: Do I really need permits for a kitchen remodel in Pacifica or Daly City?

acceptedAnswer.text: You don’t need a permit for every single kitchen update, but in Pacifica, Daly City, San Bruno, Hillsborough, and Half Moon Bay, you **do** need permits for any work that touches plumbing, electrical, gas lines, or structural elements. In general, you **don’t need a permit** for purely cosmetic changes like: - Painting walls or cabinets - Swapping out cabinet hardware - Replacing countertops with the same layout You **do need permits** when you: - Add or move electrical outlets, lighting, or circuits - Change plumbing locations (sink, dishwasher, fridge water line) - Replace or move gas ranges or gas lines - Remove or modify walls, especially if they might be load-bearing - Make major ventilation changes, like new range hoods and ducts Local cities also follow California codes that require specific things like water-saving faucets, GFCI outlets along counters, and high-efficiency lighting. If you skip permits, you risk failing future inspections, trouble when selling the home, and having to redo work. To stay safe, call your city’s building division or check their website with your project list and ask, “Do I need a permit for this?” Then confirm your contractor’s plan includes pulling the correct permits and scheduling inspections. Don’t rely on “we never pull permits for kitchens” – that’s a red flag.
